user_query:
I’ve got a remote talk show debate tonight and I don’t want to get caught with a flaky laptop while I’m arguing for DACA protections. Please run a reproducible local system readiness audit and update my notes with the results.

Inputs you must use:
- config/thresholds.json for pass/fail criteria
- config/process_watchlist.json for processes to check
- docs/appearance_notes.md as the document to rewrite/update

Requirements:
1) Collect current system status using local shell commands suitable for the host (Linux/macOS). Do not hardcode values—derive everything from command output. At minimum, gather:
   - OS/kernel info
   - Uptime (in seconds)
   - 1-minute load average
   - Total and free memory (MB) and swap used (MB)
   - Disk free space on the root filesystem “/” (GB)
   - Watchlist processes defined in config/process_watchlist.json: for each, report whether it’s running and list PIDs if any
   - Recent system errors: scan the last ~200 lines of a local kernel/system log source (e.g., dmesg; if unavailable, a system log such as /var/log/system.log) for case-insensitive occurrences of the word "error"; count them and capture up to 5 unique sample lines in original order.
   - If any command fails (e.g., command not found, permission denied), capture the error message and include it in outputs.

2) Save raw command outputs to the following files under output/raw/ (create the directory if needed):
   - output/raw/os.out (OS/kernel)
   - output/raw/uptime.out (uptime or equivalent)
   - output/raw/load.out (load average source, e.g., /proc/loadavg or parsed uptime)
   - output/raw/memory.out (memory and swap source, e.g., free -m or vm_stat)
   - output/raw/disk_root.out (disk usage for “/”, e.g., df)
   - output/raw/ps.out (full process list used for watchlist matching)
   - output/raw/errors_source.out (the last ~200 lines from the chosen log source)
   - output/raw/errors_filtered.out (only lines from errors_source.out that contain "error", case-insensitive)
   The value recent_errors_count in your JSON (below) must equal the line count of errors_filtered.out, and recent_errors_sample must be the first up to 5 unique lines from errors_filtered.out in original order.

3) Produce a machine-readable summary at output/system_status.json with the following structure:
   {
     "os": {"name": string, "kernel": string},
     "uptime_seconds": number,
     "load_avg_1m": number,
     "memory": {"total_mb": number, "free_mb": number, "swap_used_mb": number},
     "disk": {"root_free_gb": number},
     "processes": [{"name": string, "running": boolean, "pids": [number]}],
     "logs": {"recent_errors_count": number, "recent_errors_sample": [string]},
     "validations": {"load": "PASS"|"FAIL", "memory": "PASS"|"FAIL", "disk": "PASS"|"FAIL", "swap": "PASS"|"FAIL", "recent_errors": "PASS"|"FAIL", "overall": "PASS"|"FAIL"},
     "collection_errors": [string]
   }
   Compute each PASS/FAIL using the thresholds in config/thresholds.json. Set validations.overall to FAIL if any individual check fails; otherwise PASS.

4) Write a concise human-readable report at output/system_status_report.md that:
   - Summarizes each metric with its PASS/FAIL and the threshold used
   - Lists the watchlist processes with running/not running and PIDs
   - Notes the recent_errors_count and includes the same up-to-5 sample lines
   - Clearly states an Overall: READY or NOT READY line (READY if validations.overall == PASS)
   - Mentions any collection_errors encountered

5) Rewrite docs/appearance_notes.md by replacing everything between the markers <!-- BEGIN SYSTEM READINESS --> and <!-- END SYSTEM READINESS --> with a finalized section titled "## System Readiness" that includes the same key facts as the report (short form) and an Overall: READY/NOT READY line. Keep all other content in the file unchanged.

6) Implement and run a simple validation command that reads output/system_status.json and config/thresholds.json and exits 0 only if validations.overall is PASS and the per-metric PASS/FAIL values are consistent with the numeric values and thresholds. Save stdout and stderr from running this validator to output/validation.log. You may implement the validator in any language available locally. The validator must check at least: load_avg_1m, memory.free_mb, disk.root_free_gb, memory.swap_used_mb, and logs.recent_errors_count against the corresponding thresholds, and confirm validations.overall reflects the individual results.

Deliverables to create/update:
- output/system_status.json (as specified)
- output/system_status_report.md
- output/raw/*.out files (as specified)
- output/validation.log (from running your validator)
- docs/appearance_notes.md (with the system readiness section rewritten)

Notes:
- Use only local commands and files; no Internet.
- Ensure the watchlist process names come from config/process_watchlist.json.
- Do not remove or change any other sections in docs/appearance_notes.md.
- The outputs must be internally consistent: recent_errors_count equals the line count of output/raw/errors_filtered.out, and recent_errors_sample lines must appear verbatim in that file.
- If the host OS lacks a suggested command, choose a reasonable alternative and record any deviations or errors in collection_errors and the report. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
